title: Maritime Navigation and Bridge Systems Integration
entry_mechanism: Saudi maritime electronics company established through PSAP Track 3 with OEM certification
executive_summary:
Maritime navigation and bridge systems — integrated bridge systems (IBS), ECDIS (Electronic Chart Display and Information System), radar, AIS, and GMDSS equipment — require periodic installation, integration, and maintenance across Saudi Arabia's expanding commercial and government fleet. Today, all bridge systems installation and maintenance is performed by foreign-certified technicians from European and Asian OEM service companies.

Node MR-4 targets the establishment of Saudi certified maritime electronics engineers and technicians capable of performing IBS installation, ECDIS commissioning, and ongoing bridge systems maintenance for Saudi-flagged vessels. The certification pathway (FURUNO, Kongsberg, Raytheon Anschütz authorised service) is well-defined and directly accessible.

This is the maritime equivalent of DE-3 (communications equipment maintenance) — a service capability upgrade using existing OEM certification pathways, creating Saudi technical employment and reducing dependency on foreign field service teams. The Saudi maritime electronics market is estimated at $40–60 million annually in installation and service revenue.

companies.name: Kongsberg Maritime
companies.rationale: Kongsberg is the leading integrated bridge system provider with active Saudi Arabia vessel contracts. Kongsberg authorised service certification for Saudi technicians.
companies.contact: Kongsberg Maritime VP After-Sales, Middle East

companies.name: Furuno Electric
companies.rationale: Furuno produces navigation radar, ECDIS, and AIS systems widely deployed on Saudi commercial vessels. Furuno dealer and service certification programme.

companies.name: Raytheon Anschütz
companies.rationale: Raytheon Anschütz is a leading IBS provider for naval and commercial vessels. Non-ITAR German technology with authorised service programme.

companies.name: JRC (Japan Radio Company)
companies.rationale: JRC produces bridge navigation equipment widely used in the Gulf region. JRC service certification programme.

companies.name: Wärtsilä Voyage
companies.rationale: Wärtsilä Voyage provides digital navigation and fleet management systems with active Gulf market presence.
companies.contact: Wärtsilä Voyage VP Service, Middle East

partners.name: Bahri Technical Services
partners.current: Vessel technical management; some electronics maintenance using foreign technicians
partners.upgrade: Train 30 Saudi maritime electronics technicians; Kongsberg and Furuno certification; Saudi-staffed bridge systems maintenance for Bahri fleet
partners.timeline: 18 months to first certified Saudi marine electronics engineers

partners.name: Saudi Maritime Academy
partners.current: Maritime officer training; some electronics courses
partners.upgrade: Marine electronics engineering programme; Kongsberg and Furuno certified training; GMDSS operator certification; 50 graduates per year

partners.name: King Abdulaziz Port Technical Institute
partners.upgrade: Bridge systems integration and maintenance specialisation; OEM certification pathway; Saudi marine electronics company formation support

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Marine Electronics
partners.current: Some maritime electronics maintenance for Aramco offshore fleet
partners.upgrade: Extend Aramco marine electronics team to commercial vessel service; dual-use (Aramco + third party) marine electronics company formation
